tool_input.summary: No dedicated PDS-hosted “Saturn plasmoid/reconnection event catalog” dataset turned up in the PDS catalog searches (Cassini/PPI) using plasmoid/reconnection/event-catalog keywords. To assess whether plasmoid-associated mass loss can balance Enceladus as a plasma source, you’ll likely need to (a) obtain an event list from the literature (or generate one) and (b) compute mass content/outflow per event using Cassini MAG/CAPS/RPWS/MIMI time series plus SPICE geometry; Enceladus source constraints can be derived/checked using INMS/CAPS/RPWS.

candidates.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Primary dataset to identify reconnection/plasmoid signatures in Saturn’s magnetotail (field reversals, dipolarizations, flux ropes) and to time/locate events for mass-loss estimates when combined with plasma moments and geometry.

candidates.title: Cassini Magnetometer Raw Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Raw MAG provides highest-fidelity measurements useful if you need to apply your own calibration/filtering for event detection and reconnection/plasmoid boundary identification.

candidates.title: Cassini-Huygens Cassini Plasma Spectrometer (CAPS) Derived Ion Moments Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Ion density, velocity, and temperature moments can be used with MAG-defined structures to estimate plasmoid mass content and mass flux during reconnection-driven outflows.

candidates.title: Cassini RPWS Electron Densities from Upper Hybrid and Plasma Wave Frequencies
candidates.reasoning: Independent electron density estimates help constrain total plasma content inside/outside plasmoids and provide context for Enceladus torus/plasma sheet densities.

candidates.title: Cassini MIMI CHEMS Calibrated Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Energetic ion composition/fluxes can help constrain heavy-ion content (water-group) relevant to Enceladus-sourced plasma and its removal during tail reconnection events.

candidates.title: CO S INMS 3 L1A U V2.0
candidates.reasoning: INMS in-situ composition measurements during Saturn system flybys (including Enceladus encounters) support estimating/validating Enceladus neutral/ion source inputs that must be balanced by magnetospheric losses.

candidates.title: CO SS S RPWS 5 NEPROXY V1.0
candidates.reasoning: RPWS electron-density proxy product can be used to build density time series for intervals where plasmoid events are identified, complementing MAG/CAPS for mass estimates.

candidates.title: CASSINI SPICE FILES
candidates.reasoning: SPICE geometry is needed to map each event time to Cassini position and magnetotail coordinates, estimate plasmoid size/volume assumptions, and convert per-event mass to a global mass-loss rate for comparison with Enceladus source rates.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Is the mass loss inferred from the detected Saturn plasmoid/reconnection event catalog sufficient to balance Enceladus as a plasma source?

dataset.title: Cassini Magnetometer Calibrated Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains Cassini magnetometer calibrated data for the entire mission. The bundle includes 1 minute averaged and 1 second averaged data provided in Kronographic (KRTP, KSO, KSM) coordinates at Saturn, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby, Jovigraphic coordinates for the Jupiter flyby, and RTN coordinates for the entire mission, and full time resolution data provided in RTN coordinates for cruise, Kronographic (KRTP) coordinates for Saturn Tour, Geographic coordinates (GSE) for the Earth flyby,and IAU_Jupiter (J3) and JMAG XYZ (JMXYZ) for Outer Cruise. A spice metakernel collection for the 1 minute averaged data is provided as well.
